title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

title: Marc Bloch to be inducted into the Panthéon under his descendants' watchful eyes
Ten years after Brexit, the forgotten temptation of 'Frexit'
France's weakened government faces a string of crises
subhead: France's government is grappling with a particularly intense heatwave and the fallout from the rape and killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna. The crises are putting Emmanuel Macron's legacy under intense scrutiny and handing future contenders fresh attacking arguments for the 2027 presidential race.

title: Europe bets on digital euro to counter the overwhelming dominance of the US dollar
subhead: The European Parliament will examine on Tuesday the creation of a digital euro system, designed to make payments cheaper and faster and, above all, to reduce Europe's dependence on US intermediaries.

title: Ten years after MP Jo Cox's assassination, 'lessons have not been learned' in the UK
subhead: On June 16, 2016, Labour MP Jo Cox was killed by a neo-Nazi and white supremacist. Tributes to her memory have multiplied, especially from her younger sister, who took up her work and was elected in the same constituency.

title: 250 years of American independence: The birth and original sin of a new world
subhead: 'America 250' (2/13). The Declaration of Independence, adopted on July 4, 1776, still stands out for its audacity. The self-determination of a people, the consent of the governed, a commitment to shared principles of equality and the 'pursuit of happiness:' It was a revolution! And a great thirst for freedom, which nevertheless stopped at the chains of slaves.

Why the heatwave is wreaking havoc on Europe's trains
France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

title: Preliminary report on killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna points to serious failings in case handling
subhead: The report, prepared by inspectors from the justice ministry and the national gendarmerie, identified a series of mistakes and a failure to follow through on procedure by the prosecutor's office in Auch and the gendarmerie in Lectoure.
byline: Grégoire Biseau,  Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lucie Soullier

title: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.

title: '10th anniversary of Brexit referendum marks event long thought impossible in the UK: The rise of the far right'
'Marc Bloch was persecuted not only by the Nazi occupiers, but also by the French state'
